sections.summary: Document title only: Regional Standard for Tempe (CXS 313R-2013).
Administrative note stating the standard was adopted in 2013 and amended in 2015 and 2017.
Applies to tempe (as defined in Section 2) intended for human consumption and excludes tempe that have undergone further processing other than freezing.
Defines tempe as a compact, white, cake‑form product made from dehulled boiled soybeans produced by solid‑state fermentation with Rhizopus spp.

Specifies composition (soybeans and Rhizopus spp. mould mixed with cooked rice/rice bran/wheat bran as inocula) and quality requirements including organoleptic attributes (texture, colour, flavour, odour), absence of foreign matter, analytical limits (moisture, protein, lipid, crude fibre) and that containers failing these requirements are 'defective'.

States the product shall consist of soybeans (any variety) and a Rhizopus spp. mould starter (R. oligosporus, R. oryzae and/or R. stolonifer) mixed with cooked rice powder, rice bran powder and/or wheat bran powder as inocula.

Sets quality factors: tempe must be compact (not easily disintegrated), white from Rhizopus mycelium, have characteristic nutty/meaty/mushroom‑like flavour and fresh odour without ammonia, be free of foreign matter, and meet analytical limits (moisture ≤65% w/w, protein ≥15% w/w, lipid ≥7% w/w, crude fibre ≤2.5% w/w).

Describes organoleptic requirements: texture compact and not easily disintegrated, colour white from Rhizopus mycelium growth, flavour characteristic (nutty, meaty, mushroom‑like), and odour characteristic of fresh tempe without ammonia smell.

Describes organoleptic requirements: texture compact and not easily disintegrated, colour white from Rhizopus mycelium growth, flavour characteristic (nutty, meaty, mushroom‑like), and odour characteristic of fresh tempe without ammonia smell.
Requires tempe to be free from foreign matter (e.g., other beans, small stones, husk).
Specifies analytical requirements for tempe: moisture ≤65% w/w, protein ≥15% w/w, lipid ≥7% w/w, and crude fibre ≤2.5% w/w.
States that any container failing to meet the quality requirements in Section 3.2 shall be considered 'defective'.

Declares no food additives permitted (4.1) but allows processing aids to control acidity during soaking, provided such aids comply with the Guidelines on substances used as processing aids (CXG 75-2010).
Statement: no food additives are permitted for this product.
Requires compliance with the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex pesticide maximum residue limits (MRLs).
Recommends preparation and handling per the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and compliance with microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.
Requires labelling in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepacked Foods (CXS 1-1985).
Specifies that the product name shown on the label shall be 'Tempe'.
States that compliance checks shall use the methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard.
